title: ExecutionReceipt
description: The durable record that an ExecutionProof was verified and an action was executed (or refused) at a protected resource. Protocol version 1.0.0.

receipt_id.description: The unique identifier of this receipt. MUST use the 'rcpt_' prefix.

proof_id.description: The identifier of the ExecutionProof that authorised this execution.

execution_attempt_id.description: The identifier of this execution attempt. MUST use the 'exec_' prefix. Distinct from proof_id (which is the authority). Used for idempotency.

execution_mode.description: The execution mode under which the action was executed. MUST match the proof's execution_mode.

outcome.description: The execution outcome. 'EXECUTED' = the action completed successfully. 'REFUSED' = the action was refused (see refusal_id). 'PARTIAL' = the action partially completed (provider returned partial success). 'UNKNOWN' = the outcome could not be determined (provider timeout, network failure).

target.description: The target resource that executed the action. MUST match the proof's target.

action.description: The action that was executed. MUST match the proof's action.

provider_response_summary.description: Optional. A summary of the provider's response. MUST NOT contain sensitive data (credentials, PII). Used for audit.

refusal_id.description: Present if outcome is 'REFUSED'. The identifier of the ExecutionRefusal. MUST use the 'rful_' prefix.

evidence_links.description: Optional. Evidence links (transparency log entries, receipt chain entries, counter-signatures) associated with this receipt.

resource_signature.description: Optional. The protected resource's signature over the canonicalised receipt payload. Required for tamper-evident receipt chains.
